_pin_rlp_to_zero_note: MUST stay false. Flag exists only for A/B backtest comparison.
When true: REPLACEMENT-tier $ is pinned to $0 (PR #11 behavior).
When false: REPLACEMENT gets formula-$ (signed-z * $/Z), same as ROSTERED and BELOW.
A/B run 2026-05-28 (PR #12, all 5 sources x hitters+pitchers):
$0-count under pinned vs formula:
updated:    197 vs  70  (2.8x more zeros pinned)
preseason:  169 vs  64  (2.6x)
synthetic:  154 vs  53  (2.9x)
current:    210 vs  52  (4.0x)
Pinning collapses 100-160 extra players per source to $0, hiding signal
across 60-80% of every pool. Examples (updated source):
Corey Seager:    formula REPLACEMENT $4.34   vs pinned $0.00
Trea Turner:     formula REPLACEMENT $8.92   vs pinned $0.00
Brayan Rocchio:  formula REPLACEMENT $8.78   vs pinned $0.00
Josh Naylor:     formula ROSTERED    $8.26   vs pinned RLP $0.00
Second-order effect: swap-pass uses $-comparison for rostered <-> RLP
swaps. Pinned RLP=$0 changes swap dynamics -> different rostered cohort
-> different rostered-tier stdev -> different $/Z. So flipping the flag
is not a pure display toggle; it reshapes which players end up rostered.
Budget conservation ($2805 across rostered) holds in both modes.
Rationale (user, 2026-05-28): '$0 means nothing if 90% of league is $0.'
